findings.issue: Wave 2: Task 4 and Task 5 both modify `app/globals.css`. Parallel agents will produce merge conflicts.
findings.fix: Move globals.css CSS removal to a single task (e.g., Task 5 removes all remaining CSS after Task 4 is done), or move Task 5's globals.css changes into Wave 3 after Task 4 completes.

findings.issue: Wave 3: Task 8 and Task 9 both modify `components/RightSidebar.tsx`. Parallel agents will produce merge conflicts.
findings.fix: Consolidate RightSidebar changes into one task. Either Task 8 handles all RightSidebar modifications (tag links + dynamic counts), or move Task 9's RightSidebar work into Task 8.

findings.issue: 17 tasks exceeds the maximum limit of 15.
findings.fix: Merge related tasks. Suggestions: (1) Merge Task 16 (Deploy) and Task 17 (Post-deploy verification) into one task. (2) Merge Task 10 (Navbar/Footer cleanup + static pages) with Task 4 (Navbar Tailwind migration) since Task 10 already depends on Task 4.

findings.issue: User-spec verification table uses port 3100 (`curl http://localhost:3100`), but tech-spec AVP uses port 3001 (`curl http://localhost:3001`). Agents will use the wrong port.
findings.fix: Align to one port. Check next.config.mjs or package.json for the actual dev port and use that consistently in the AVP section.

findings.issue: Patterns.md states all content starts as `pending` and requires admin approval. Tech-spec (and user-spec) say sync writes `published` directly. This contradicts Project Knowledge without documenting it as a deviation.
findings.fix: Add a Decision entry documenting the choice to skip moderation in MVP (sync → published directly). Or add it to User-Spec Deviations section noting the PK override.

findings.issue: Tasks 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 9, 11 use `code-writing` skill but omit `security-auditor` from reviewers. Per skills-and-reviewers.md, the default reviewer set for `code-writing` is [code-reviewer, security-auditor, test-reviewer].
findings.fix: Add `security-auditor` to reviewers for these tasks, or explicitly justify the omission (e.g., no user input handling, no DB queries).

findings.issue: Tasks 4 and 5 use `Verify-user` instead of `Verify-smoke`. Inconsistent field naming may confuse automation.
findings.fix: Rename to `Verify-smoke` for consistency, or define both field types explicitly if `Verify-user` is intentional (manual check vs automated smoke).

findings.issue: Architecture.md references `lib/db.ts` as Prisma singleton, but project actually uses `lib/prisma.ts`. Tech-spec correctly references `lib/prisma.ts`, but PK is outdated.
findings.fix: Update architecture.md to reference `lib/prisma.ts` (separate task or note for documentation).

findings.issue: Tasks 12-14 (audit tasks) lack `Files to read` field. Auditors need to know which files were created/modified to review.
findings.fix: Add `Files to read: all files modified/created in Tasks 1-11` or list them explicitly.

summary: Tech-spec has 3 critical findings blocking approval: two wave conflicts (globals.css in Wave 2, RightSidebar in Wave 3) and task count exceeding the 15-task limit (currently 17). Additionally, there are 3 major findings: port mismatch between user-spec and tech-spec AVP, undocumented PK deviation on content moderation flow, and missing security-auditor reviewer on 7 code-writing tasks. Fix the 3 critical issues to reach approved status.
